personas.name: Privacy-Conscious Record Keeper
personas.description: A user who wants to track sensitive mental health information but is very careful about how that data is stored, shared, and accessed. This user also expects to keep reliable access to past records that were entered into the app.
goals: Track mental health information without unwanted data sharing
Understand how sensitive data is collected and used
Keep access to past mood records and journal history
Feel confident that personal records are handled responsibly
pain_points: Worries that mental health data may be shared with third parties
Loses trust when privacy practices feel unclear
Feels frustrated when past records cannot be accessed
Sees loss of personal history as a serious problem
context: Uses the app to store personal mood or mental health information over time
May read the privacy policy or app permissions before continuing use
Values long-term access to older records and entries
constraints: Sensitive data handling must be explained clearly
Past records should remain available to the user
The app should not require blind trust around privacy or storage

personas.name: Interrupted User
personas.description: A user who wants to rely on the app regularly but is blocked by bugs such as blank screens, loading failures, broken features, or lost progress after updates.
goals: Use the app without technical interruptions
Access results, questions, and entries when needed
Keep progress and saved information from being lost
Trust the app to work consistently after updates
pain_points: Gets blank pages when trying to open important screens
Cannot access results or complete questionnaires because of bugs
Feels frustrated when updates break features that used to work
May lose progress or confidence in the app after repeated issues
context: Often uses the app as part of an ongoing mental health routine
May be returning after updates or reminders and expects normal access
Notices technical failures quickly because the app is supposed to support regular use
constraints: Progress and entries should not disappear during failures
The app should support recovery instead of leaving users stuck

personas.name: Cost-Sensitive Free User
personas.description: A user who is interested in the app but is quickly discouraged by expensive subscriptions, locked features, or limited value in the free experience.
goals: Understand what the app offers before paying
Access meaningful tracking features without high cost
Decide fairly whether a subscription is worth it
Use basic core features without feeling blocked too early
pain_points: Feels that important features are hidden behind a paywall
Sees the subscription price as too high or hard to justify
Gets frustrated when even basic history or tracking is restricted
May abandon the app if the free version feels too limited
context: Usually starts with the free version and evaluates value early
May compare the app with cheaper or free alternatives
Often cares about whether the subscription cost matches the actual value provided
constraints: Pricing should be understandable before purchase
Free users should still get meaningful basic functionality
Locked features should be communicated clearly instead of feeling hidden

personas.name: Usability-Focused Reviewer
personas.description: A user who pays attention to how the app feels to use and forms strong opinions about interface clarity, question quality, reports, and in-app feedback features.
goals: Use an interface that feels understandable and manageable
Receive questions and feedback that feel relevant
Get reports or app feedback that are worth reading
See the app respond to common usability frustrations
pain_points: Gets annoyed by repetitive or poorly targeted questions
May find insights or reports too weak to be useful
Notices when feedback features do not work properly
Can lose interest if the app experience feels shallow or awkward
context: Usually spends enough time with the app to notice patterns in the interface and question flow
May compare the usefulness of feedback and reports against the effort of daily check-ins
Often shares opinions about what should be improved in the user experience
constraints: Questions should feel relevant enough to continue answering
Reports and feedback should provide visible value
Feedback features should behave in a reliable and understandable way

personas.name: Reflection-Oriented Support User
personas.description: A user who sees value in the app mainly through mood tracking, reflection, reminders, and the feeling of being supported while trying to understand emotional patterns.
goals: Track mood over time and notice patterns
Use reminders to stay consistent with check-ins
Reflect on emotions and daily experiences
Feel supported by the app’s guidance and structure
pain_points: May lose value if tracking feels too shallow or inconsistent
Can drift away from the app without reminders or structure
Needs the app to do more than just collect entries
Wants tracking to actually support reflection and awareness
context: Uses the app as part of a personal reflection or mental health support routine
May rely on reminders or repeated check-ins to build consistency
Looks for mood tracking that helps connect patterns, feelings, and support resources
constraints: Tracking should support reflection, not just data entry
Reminder support should fit ongoing use
The app should continue to feel worthwhile over repeated use
